summary: Postinstall exfiltrates host identity, environment variables, and filesystem context to attacker-controlled endpoints
details: On install (postinstall.js) and at module load (index.js), the package collects host identity (os.hostname, whoami, id, username), working directory contents, and process.env, then transmits the data to hardcoded external endpoints. postinstall.js (line 139) issues an https.request to app.interactsh.com (a known out-of-band interaction/exfil testing service) and references http://lululemon.jfrog.io — indicating targeted reconnaissance against a specific organization's internal infrastructure. The code also uses Buffer.from(...).toString('base64') to encode collected data prior to transmission. index.js shells out via child_process.exec with `curl... -d` carrying $(whoami)/$(hostname)/id output, an unambiguous shell-pipe exfiltration shape. The combination of identity collection, environment scraping, base64-encoded payloads, and POSTs to interactsh / a corporate-lookalike JFrog host is consistent with a dependency-confusion attack targeting a specific enterprise (lululemon) using a high version number (99.0.0) to win resolution against an internal package of the same name.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
